task_summary: Repair the v0.1.0 foreign-distribution mesh-store fingerprint
trigger_reason: v0.1.0 queried logicguard distribution metadata and bound version 0.18.0 into current ResearchGuard durable identity

commands.command: focused package identity and residual tests

findings: mesh-store durable identity is owned only by current researchguard.__version__
predecessor-present and predecessor-absent scenarios resolve the same identity without a metadata query
retired imports metadata queries and declared dependencies are rejection-only
counterexamples: v0.1.0 produced a mesh-store fingerprint with logicguard package version 0.18.0 while ResearchGuard was 0.1.0
friction_points: predictive-KB preflight failed closed because the current Chaos Brain maintenance standard was not committed; no fallback retrieval was attempted
skipped_steps: native member tests installation and publication remain separate required gates
next_actions: run all member-native checks and the complete test suite
publish and audit v0.1.1 six-face identity
claim_boundary: This entry proves only the executable model-miss repair and focused checks; it does not replace native member tests installation or publication evidence.

task_summary: Repair the SourceGuard content-anchor native blocking-code drift
trigger_reason: The catalog-declared content-anchor oracle required sourceguard_blocked:contentless-anchor while the native depth owner emitted no_content_qualified_anchor, preventing exact target-purpose proof closure

commands.summary: aligned good and bad cases passed; drifted code produced the expected invariant violation

findings: The SourceGuard oracle catalog is the stable fingerprint-bound contract authority.
The native depth finding was the sole drifted owner and was replaced directly with sourceguard_blocked:contentless-anchor.
Runtime and test residuals of no_content_qualified_anchor are zero; no alias, dual emission, or fallback was added.
All four SkillGuard member closures are enforced and current for the frozen v0.1.3 source.
counterexamples: The v0.1.2 contentless-anchor bad case blocked depth but could not close its target-purpose proof because exact finding identity comparison observed no_content_qualified_anchor instead of sourceguard_blocked:contentless-anchor.
friction_points: The project FlowGuard adoption record was upgraded from package 0.58.4 to 0.58.5 before modeling.
One outer SkillGuard wait window expired while the owned SourceGuard run continued; the exact process tree reached zero and the same terminal passing result was inspected without retry.
skipped_steps: The narrow child model intentionally leaves progress, contract, conformance, and counterexample minimization not run because those optional relations are outside the finite finding-identity claim; native tests and suite checks provide the required runtime evidence.
Installation and GitHub publication are separate release gates and are not proven by this FlowGuard entry.
next_actions: verify and archive the OpenSpec change
install the exact v0.1.3 consumer projection
publish and audit the v0.1.3 GitHub identity
claim_boundary: This entry proves the modeled finding-identity repair, affected checks, full suite, and frozen author-side member closures. It does not by itself prove installation, publication, source truth, or future AI behavior.
